frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, idhellam too much da. Just because one or two incidents happen, why do all women generalize and assume all men are potential threats? I mean, I've always been respectful, but suddenly I feel like I'm being judged too. What about us good guys?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment expresses 'Not All Men' dismissal by challenging women's generalization ('why do all women generalize') and expressing personal offense ('I feel like I'm being judged too'). It centers the speaker's perceived 'goodness' ('I've always been respectful') while dismissing women's wariness as an irrational overreaction. The Tamil interjections 'Yaar, idhellam too much da' add authenticity for a Chennai context.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 34. A sales manager in Chennai. You are texting your male college friends group on WhatsApp after your company announced paid period leave for female employees. You are commenting on whether period leave is fair to male colleagues.
